ids._model: Cumulative-item tiers under 37 CFR 1.17(v) (effective 2025-01-19). The fee is a one-time, cumulative, large-entity amount keyed to the TOTAL number of items of information cited across all IDSs in the application; a later tier is reduced by amounts already paid in earlier tiers. APA does not auto-bill these; it surfaces the tier table for human review.

tiers.label: IDS size fee - cumulative items exceeding 50 but not exceeding 100
IDS size fee - cumulative items exceeding 100 but not exceeding 200 (less amounts previously paid)
IDS size fee - cumulative items exceeding 200 (less amounts previously paid)

prosecution._model: 37 CFR 1.17(a) extension-of-time fees (LARGE entity, effective 2025-01-19), used by apa-prosecute for 1.136(a) response-deadline estimates. The fee is for the TOTAL extension length (NOT additive per month). For a standard 3-month shortened statutory period only tiers 1-3 are usable; a 4th/5th-month total response would exceed the 35 USC 133 six-month statutory maximum, so tiers 4-5 apply only when the shortened period is shorter than 3 months. Apply the entity multiplier; APA surfaces these as estimates to verify. Source: third_party/uspto-references/uspto-filing-and-fees.md (37 CFR 1.17(a)).

_note: ESTIMATE; verify against the live USPTO fee schedule (37 CFR 1.16/1.17). Amounts are LARGE-entity; multiply by entityMultipliers. IMPORTANT MULTIPLIER CORRECTION: the originally-templated multipliers of small=0.50/micro=0.25 are OUT OF DATE. The Unleashing American Innovators Act of 2022 raised the discounts to small=60%/micro=80%, so a small entity now pays 0.40 and a micro entity 0.20 of the large-entity fee (effective 2025-01-19). These corrected multipliers (0.40/0.20) are confirmed against the fetched USPTO fee reference (third_party/uspto-references/uspto-filing-and-fees.md, 37 CFR 1.16/1.17) and are no longer flagged unverified; statute/regulation still governs - verify currency before relying. The IDS size-based fee (37 CFR 1.17(v)) is a cumulative, one-time fee keyed to total items cited and is provided as a reference table only; APA does not compute or assert it automatically. APA never asserts micro-entity status (37 CFR 1.29 is a human certification, not an APA determination).
